description: The agent always has an accurate, sourced reference for what every connected integration can do and what permissions it needs — created the moment a new integration appears, refreshed whenever the user asks. No scheduled routine.

customize.description: Directory where integration capabilities-and-scopes files are stored in the brain.

changelog.notes: Removed the nightly maintenance routine; sync is on-demand and event-driven via the user.md snippet. The skill now runs only when a new integration appears (behavior snippet) or when the user asks ('document my integrations', 'sync the capabilities file for X'). Cut the Nightly Routine Content Template from the skill and the post-install reminder-add cron step from the README. The skill never creates a scheduled routine; users who want a periodic refresh can ask Runneth to schedule one themselves.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.
Initial release. Integration capabilities-and-scopes library with nightly sync skill, brain index, and new-integration detection behavior.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.
